Package org.gbif.utils.file.tabular

Interface TabularDataFileReader<T>

All Superinterfaces:
AutoCloseable, Closeable
public interface TabularDataFileReader<T> extends Closeable
Interface defining a reader for tabular data file (e.g. CSV)

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    List<String>
    getHeaderLine()
    Get the header line of the tabular data file (if possible).
    long
    getLastRecordLineNumber()
    The line number of where the last record returned by read() starts.
    long
    getLastRecordNumber()
    Represents the number of record returned by the read() method.
    T
    read()
    Read a non-empty line of the tabular data file.

    Methods inherited from interface java.io.Closeable

    close

  • Method Details

    • getHeaderLine

      List<String> getHeaderLine() throws IOException
      Get the header line of the tabular data file (if possible).
      Returns:
      headers or null
      Throws:
      IOException

    • read

      T read() throws IOException, ParseException
      Read a non-empty line of the tabular data file. An empty line represents a line with no printable characters. A line with only the defined separators is expected to be returned.
      Returns:
      the next line of the tabular data file or null if the end of the file is reached.
      Throws:
      IOException
      ParseException

    • getLastRecordLineNumber

      long getLastRecordLineNumber()
      The line number of where the last record returned by read() starts. If no records have been returned yet this method is expected to return 0. Once read() returned null, this methods will return the number of the last line in the file. Note that if the very last line is an empty line with only an endline character it will not be counted. Line number includes header line and empty lines (if applicable).
      Returns:
      line number of where the last record starts

    • getLastRecordNumber

      long getLastRecordNumber()
      Represents the number of record returned by the read() method. If no records have been returned yet this method is expected to return 0. A record can span over multiple line.
      Returns:
